title: Boat Rental in Amalfi: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Boat hire along the Amalfi Coast opens up dramatic cliffs, hidden coves and pastel villages only reachable by sea. From the port of Amalfi itself, you can rent a boat for a day or a week, with prices starting from around €300 for a half-day motorboat. BoatHire24 connects you with local operators to make planning straightforward.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Amalfi?

Prices vary widely, from about €150 for a shared tour to over €2,000 for a crewed yacht per day. A small motorboat for two to four people half day costs roughly €300 to €500. Catamarans for eight to twelve guests start around €1,200 for a full day, while a private crewed motor yacht such as an Azimut 60 can reach €2,500. Always confirm fuel and mooring fees separately as these can add 20 per cent. For the best choice of vessels at every price point, visit this dedicated page for boat hire Amalfi and compare verified boat listings.

Where to set off in Amalfi

Most charters depart from the Marina d’Amalfi, the main harbour in town, though you can also join tours from Marina di Praia, a smaller cove near the Grotta dello Smeraldo. Positano’s Porto di Positano is another popular departure point, especially for trips to the Li Galli islands or Capri. For those staying west, Salerno’s Marina d’Arechi is a modern base. When sailing east, the bay at Maiori offers a calm launch spot. Each of these marinas has ferry and charter facilities; the distance from Amalfi to Capri by boat is about 15 nautical miles.

Which boat to rent in Amalfi?

  1. Small motorboat Best for couples or small groups exploring coves such as Furore and Fornillo; seats 4, 6; from €350 half-day.
  2. Catamaran Ideal for family or friends wanting space and stability; seats 8, 12; from €1,200 full-day.
  3. Luxury motor yacht For a premium experience with a skipper; seats up to 10; from €1,800 full-day; consider an Azimut or Fairline.
  4. Classic gozzo A traditional wooden boat popular for sunset tours; seats 6, 8; from €250 for two hours.
  5. RIB (rigid inflatable boat) Fast and nimble for reaching Capri in under an hour; seats 6; from €400 half-day.

For a detailed look at one premium option, refer to the Mefasa 90 listing, a 90-foot luxury motor yacht available for charter from Amalfi.

Cost breakdown for Amalfi boat hire

  • Shared group tour (up to 12 people): €80, €150 per person for a day trip to Capri.
  • Small motorboat half-day (2, 4 people): €300, €500 without fuel.
  • Catamaran full-day (8, 12 people): €1,200, €1,800 including skipper.
  • Crewed motor yacht full-day (10 people): €1,800, €3,000 with crew and lunch.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Amalfi?

Italian law requires a patente nautica for boats over 40 horsepower; smaller, unlicensed boats are available. Most hire companies offer tiller-steered boats under 40 hp without a licence, typically seating up to six. For larger craft, you need an ICC (International Certificate of Competence) or Italian licence. The operator will confirm exact requirements when you book.
A local tip: the summer Scirocco wind can kick up a sudden chop in the afternoon, so return earlier in the day and explore early morning when the sea is glassy.

Season for boat rental in Amalfi in 2026

The peak season runs from June through August, when weather is settled and water temperatures exceed 22 °C. July and August are busiest and most expensive; May, June and September offer quieter cruising. The prevailing breeze is the Ponente from the west, which along the coast creates a reliable afternoon wind for sailing. Some operators run charters from mid-April to late October, but confirm availability directly.

At a glance

  • Price band: €150, €3,000 per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: 4, 8 hours typical
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests depending on vessel
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Amalfi

Can I rent a boat in Amalfi without a skipper?

Yes, many small motorboats under 40 hp can be hired self-drive without a licence for up to six people.

How far is Capri from Amalfi by boat?

The crossing takes about 45 to 60 minutes from Amalfi port depending on your boat’s speed.

Is it safe to anchor near the Amalfi coast?

Yes, but avoid protected marine areas near Punta Campanella and always respect no-anchor buoys.

What is the best month in 2026 to rent a boat in Amalfi?

June and September offer warm weather with fewer crowds and milder winds than July and August.

Whether you are planning a day trip to the Li Galli islands or a week exploring the coast, the Amalfi coast is best seen from the water.
